title: BUG #18496: Strange Handling for Circular Views and Permissions in PostgreSQL
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE test_table (id INT AUTO_INCREMENT PRIMARY KEY, name VARCHAR(50), value INT CHECK (value >= 0));
INSERT INTO test_table (name, value) VALUES ('test1', 10), ('test2', NULL), ('test3', 0);
CREATE VIEW circular_view AS SELECT id, name FROM test_table WHERE value = (SELECT value FROM test_table WHERE id = 1);
CREATE VIEW circular_view_2 AS SELECT * FROM circular_view WHERE id IN (SELECT id FROM circular_view);
key_bug_features: SELECT on circular view without permissions
